records.ety: [OE. avocat, avocet, OF. avocat, fr. L. advocatus, one summoned or called to another; properly the p. p. of advocare to call to, call to one's aid; ad + vocare to call. See Advowee, Avowee, Vocal.]

defs: One who pleads the cause of another. Specifically: One who pleads the cause of another before a tribunal or judicial court; a counselor.
notes: ☞ In the English and American Law, advocate is the same as “counsel,” “counselor,” or “barrister.” In the civil and ecclesiastical courts, the term signifies the same as “counsel” at the common law.

defs: One who defends, vindicates, or espouses any cause by argument; a pleader; as, an advocate of free trade, an advocate of truth.

quotes: We have an Advocate with the Father.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Advocated (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Advocating (<?/).]

defs: To plead in favor of; to defend by argument, before a tribunal or the public; to support, vindicate, or recommend publicly.
quotes: To advocate the cause of thy client.
This is the only thing distinct and sensible, that has been advocated.
Eminent orators were engaged to advocate his cause.
